steps.title: Use the likely pattern
steps.content: The sentence says respondents were more likely to do something. In standard English, the adjective likely is followed by an infinitive: likely to eat, likely to go, likely to happen.

steps.title: Reject non-infinitive forms
steps.content: Choice A is a simple past verb. Choice B is a future progressive verb. Choice D is a past participle. None completes the adjective likely correctly. The remaining choice supplies the needed infinitive before common Brazilian foods. The answer is C.
